item.title: Optically Anisotropic Self-Organised ZnO:Al Eutectic Metamaterial for Mid-Infrared Plasmonics
item.description:
Communications Materials, Published online: 09 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s43246-026-01265-z

Plasmonic materials in the mid-infrared have great potential for applications such as atmospheric and biological sensing, heat transfer, and photothermal conversion. Here, the authors develop an anisotropic metamaterial using ZnO:Al layers in a ZnWO₄ matrix, achieving tunable epsilon-near-zero resonances via light polarisation, paving the way for scalable, versatile infrared applications like sensors and filters.
